verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP5B) is the catalytic beta subunit of mitochondrial F1Fo ATP synthase, with its dominant localization at the inner mitochondrial membrane. However, multiple independent lines of evidence document an ectopic surface pool (ecto-ATP synthase) on tumor, endothelial, and neuronal cells: the complex is physically trafficked from mitochondria to the plasma membrane via microtubule/DRP1/KIF5B, and the mitochondrial membrane fuses to the PM to anchor it. RNA aptamers and antibodies targeting surface ATP5B are validated therapeutic leads in breast and prostate cancer. Surface expression is cell-state/tumor-context dependent, not the universal baseline, making this contextual rather than yes.
